startup_description: Automated maintenance scheduling and tracking tool for property managers juggling 10-50 rental properties. Eliminates missed maintenance, reduces tenant complaints, and prevents costly emergency repairs through proactive scheduling.

hero_answers.Q1_problem_customer: Property managers with 10-50 rental units struggle to track recurring maintenance tasks across all properties. They miss HVAC filter changes, annual inspections, seasonal maintenance, and lease renewal deadlines. This leads to tenant complaints, emergency repair costs 3x higher than preventive maintenance, and liability issues when safety inspections lapse. Current solution is manual calendar reminders that don't scale past 10 properties.
Q2_target_user: Independent property managers managing 10-50 residential rental units across multiple properties
Small property management companies (2-5 employees) without enterprise software budgets
Real estate investors self-managing their own rental portfolio
hero_answers.Q3_success_metric: Within 90 days of launch: 50 property manager signups, 20 active users who added at least 5 properties and scheduled 10+ maintenance tasks, 6 paying subscribers at $39-79/month tier, 300+ maintenance tasks scheduled in system proving core workflow works.
Q4_must_have_features: User registration and login - Email/password auth for property managers
Property portfolio - Add properties with address, unit count, key dates (purchase date, last major maintenance)
Maintenance task library - Pre-built templates for common tasks (HVAC filter change every 90 days, annual fire extinguisher inspection, seasonal gutter cleaning)
Recurring schedule builder - Set task frequency (monthly, quarterly, annually, custom) and auto-generate future instances
Task assignment - Assign tasks to properties/units with due dates and mark complete with notes and photos
Email reminders - Automated emails 7 days before task due, day of due date, and 3 days after overdue
Q5_non_goals: Contractor marketplace or vendor management system
Tenant communication portal or maintenance requests from tenants
Accounting integration or invoice tracking for maintenance costs
Work order dispatch system for maintenance crews
IoT device integration for smart home monitoring
Lease management or rent collection features
Multi-user team collaboration with role permissions

Q6_constraints.brand_positioning: Dead simple maintenance calendar that scales with your portfolio
Q6_constraints.scope: Maintenance scheduling and tracking only - not a full property management suite
Q6_constraints.economics: Monthly subscription $39-79/month based on property count, target 40% gross margin after $15-25/user operational costs
Q6_constraints.build_time: 75 days total - 60 days build, 15 days user testing with 3-5 beta property managers
Q6_constraints.tech_requirements: Single-user accounts (each property manager has isolated data), recurring task logic, photo uploads for task completion, email scheduling

Q7_data_sources: User manual input - Property addresses, unit counts, custom maintenance tasks, completion notes
Task library seed data - Pre-populate 20-30 common property maintenance tasks with recommended frequencies
System-generated - Task instance scheduling based on recurrence rules, reminder schedules
Q8_integrations: SendGrid or AWS SES - Transactional email for task reminders and overdue notifications (required for MVP)
Stripe - Subscription billing for property managers (required for MVP)
Twilio (Phase 2) - SMS reminders for time-sensitive tasks
Google Calendar API (Phase 2) - Sync tasks to user's personal calendar
Vendor APIs (Phase 2) - Integration with contractor scheduling tools
Q9_risks: Property managers are late adopters of technology - may prefer pen and paper or simple spreadsheets
Email reminders getting marked as spam if sending volume is high - need proper email domain reputation
Photo upload for task completion could have storage cost implications at scale - cap at 5 photos per task, 2MB each
Recurring task logic bugs (especially around edge cases like Feb 29, daylight savings) could break trust
Competition from full property management suites like Buildium, AppFolio (but they're $200-400/month, we're targeting the underserved $39-79 market)
hero_answers.Q10_shipping_preference: Start with property management, task library, recurring schedule builder, and email reminders. Focus on nailing the recurring task logic and making task completion workflow fast (under 30 seconds to mark complete and add notes). Skip contractor management, tenant portals, and accounting until v2.

external_apis: S3 or Cloudinary (photo storage for task completion)
